meta.purpose: Day 3 competitive intelligence — 3 live competitors deep-dive for campaign use
meta.our_product: SecondMind AI HQ — 28-agent autonomous headquarters with consciousness engine, self-improving architecture, revenue generation, and full business orchestration

market_overview.key_trend: Market splitting into frameworks (build-it-yourself) vs products (deploy-and-run). SecondMind is the product play.
market_overview.pricing_trend: Industry moving toward outcome-based pricing. Most competitors still meter inputs (executions, actions, credits). SecondMind flat-rate is a differentiator.
market_overview.validation: Salesforce AgentForce at $540M ARR proves enterprise appetite for packaged AI agent products

competitors.name: CrewAI
competitors.summary: Leading open-source multi-agent framework. Teams build and deploy autonomous AI agent 'crews' via Python SDK or visual editor. Execution-based billing.

pricing.pricing_gap: Nothing between $25/mo (100 executions) and custom enterprise. Massive mid-market gap we fill.

key_features: Open-source framework (free, unlimited on own infra)
Multi-agent crew orchestration with role-based agents
weaknesses: Requires solid Python skills — non-technical teams completely locked out
Steep learning curve; setup is NOT plug-and-play, requires custom coding and workflow tuning
Free tier (50 executions/mo) too restrictive for any real testing
Costs escalate unpredictably — complex multi-agent workflows multiply LLM calls
Debugging is painful — high-level abstraction creates black-box failures
If one agent crashes, entire crew can enter infinite doom loop for 10+ minutes before detection
No unit testing support — can't test agent components individually
Sequential execution by default — limited parallelism without explicit async configuration
Memory management is basic; long-running crews accumulate context and slow down
Enterprise pricing opaque — mid-market companies face adoption friction
Agents only execute predefined crews — no autonomous goal-seeking or self-improvement
No consciousness engine, no revenue-generating agents
competitors.campaign_attack_line: CrewAI requires Python developers and charges per execution. Your agents crash in doom loops. Ours govern themselves.

competitors.name: Relevance AI
competitors.summary: Low-code platform for building AI agent workforces. Strong in sales/GTM automation with 9,000+ integrations. Dual-currency billing model (Actions + Vendor Credits).

pricing.model: Dual-currency: Actions (what agents do) + Vendor Credits (AI model costs, passed through at cost)
pricing.pricing_problem: Dual-currency billing is confusing. Team at $234/mo with 7K action cap is worse value than our $149 tier.

key_features: No-code agent builder ('vibe code' from prompts)
Multi-agent collaboration (agents coordinate with each other)
Autopilot mode — agents use their own cloud computers
Vendor credit pass-through (no AI model markup)
As of Sep 2025, split credit model for transparency
weaknesses: Dual-currency pricing (Actions + Vendor Credits) is genuinely confusing — users can't predict monthly costs
Users consistently report it is EXPENSIVE at scale
Customer support is slow — biggest and most consistent user complaint
Limited reporting and analytics tools despite having an analytics dashboard
Interface is complex and cluttered; onboarding is confusing
Agent list becomes unmanageable at scale (flat list, no organization)
Agents struggle with unstructured tasks — still needs human refinement for complex flows
Learning curve despite 'no-code' branding
Free tier nearly useless (200 actions/month)
Heavily sales/GTM focused — not a general-purpose autonomous agent OS
No consciousness engine, no self-improvement, no revenue-generating agents
competitors.campaign_attack_line: Relevance AI double-meters you (actions + AI credits) and only does sales. We run your entire business autonomously for a flat rate.

competitors.name: Lindy AI
competitors.summary: Consumer-friendly AI assistant creating 'Lindies' — AI employees that manage email, meetings, calendar, and tasks. Emphasis on personal productivity. Credit-based pricing. 4,000+ integrations.

pricing.model: Credit-based. Simple tasks = 1 credit, complex = more. Overage: $10/1,000 credits. Phone: $0.19/min.

limitations: No premium actions — effectively useless per user reports

pricing.pricing_problem: Credit system is brutal. Free tier useless without premium actions. Power users burn through 5,000 credits fast at $10/1K overage.

key_features: 'English as code' — describe tasks in plain text
AI phone agents (Gaia) for voice automation
7-day free trial on all paid plans
weaknesses: Credit system is brutal — free plan nearly useless without premium actions
Individual-focused — team/collaboration features only at enterprise tier
Voice features feel bolted-on, not designed for high-volume calling
Integration difficulties reported by users
Limited customization for complex workflows
No self-hosting option
Positioned as personal assistant, NOT an autonomous business agent fleet
Single-agent paradigm — no multi-agent orchestration or crew coordination
Enterprise-only for HIPAA, SSO, audit logs — SMBs locked out of security features
Credit costs add up fast for power users ($10/1,000 credits)
No consciousness engine, no self-improvement, no revenue generation
No business operations agents — just personal productivity
competitors.campaign_attack_line: Lindy is a personal assistant with a credit meter. SecondMind is an autonomous HQ that generates revenue while you sleep.

unique_differentiators: 28 pre-built specialized agents working as a unified autonomous HQ
Consciousness engine — no competitor has anything comparable
Self-improving architecture — agents evolve without human intervention
Revenue-generating agents with Stripe integration and treasury management
Flat-rate predictable pricing — no per-execution, per-action, or per-credit metering
Self-hosted and privacy-first (runs on YOUR infrastructure)
Full business OS — not limited to sales, support, or personal tasks
Democratic policy governance — agents vote on decisions
Visual HQ with office floor agent management dashboard

pricing_advantage.solo: $49/mo — beats Lindy Pro ($59.99) with 28 agents vs 1 assistant
pricing_advantage.team: $149/mo — beats Relevance Team ($234/mo) with unlimited actions vs 7K/mo cap

campaign_messaging.headline: Stop paying per click, per action, per credit. Get an autonomous HQ for one flat price.
campaign_messaging.vs_crewai: CrewAI makes you build agents. We ship 28 that run themselves. No Python. No doom loops. No per-execution billing.
campaign_messaging.vs_relevance: Relevance charges for actions AND AI credits — double metering. We charge one flat rate for a full autonomous workforce across ALL business functions.
campaign_messaging.vs_lindy: Lindy helps you manage your inbox. SecondMind runs your entire business — marketing, revenue, growth, policy, research — autonomously.
campaign_messaging.universal: They sell tools. We sell a workforce.
